metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that child-4 ticket `rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8` exists and defines the exact gate-aware `calibrate_criterion` / `eval_solver` extension this ticket's dispatch targets; if the ticket was renamed or merged, update the reference and confirm the code-review solver arm will be present before this ticket ships.
evidence: Ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 returns 'not found' from the ticket store.
`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:384-512` — `calibrate_criterion` has no gate-aware branching; it calls `_criterion_solver` which calls `eval_solver.run_case`.

`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:66-88` — `_criterion_id` only checks `plan_review.registry.by_id()`; a code-review project id returns `None` and falls to the `code-review-` prefix arm (which requires a `code-review-` prefixed prompt id, not `project.foo`).
ABSENCE: no `gate` parameter or code-review branch in `calibrate_criterion` or `_criterion_solver`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that child 4 `[rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]` adds a 'gate-aware calibrate path' to `calibrate_criterion`, but the ticket cannot be found and no such code-review dispatch path exists in the current `calibrate_criterion` / `_criterion_solver` / `eval_solver._criterion_id` chain. The entire dispatch contract this ticket's work relies on is an unbacked premise.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'
scenarios: After this ticket's CLI changes admit a code-review project id, `calibrate_criterion` is called with it. `_criterion_solver` builds a `solve` fn that calls `eval_solver.run_case('project.foo', ...)`, which finds no matching arm (not plan-review, not `code-review-` prefix) and raises `ValueError: no eval solver for prompt 'project.foo'`.
If child 4 has not landed, the entire happy-path AC fails at runtime despite the preflight fix being correct.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm which ticket in the stack creates `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`; if no other ticket owns it, add its creation explicitly to this ticket's scope (not just extension).
evidence: Directory listing of `tests/unit/` shows no `test_code_review_project_criteria.py` file.
AC lines 1–3 all reference `-k cli` in `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py`.
ABSENCE: file is not present and no other ticket in this conversation creates it.
findings.finding: The plan refers to `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` as a 'shared test file created earlier in the stack', but this file does not exist in the repo. All three AC items are verified by `-k cli` against this file, so if it isn't created by a parent/sibling ticket, the test target is absent and the AC cannot be validated.
findings.location: Scope section — 'tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py — extend this shared test file (created earlier in the stack)'
scenarios: `rtk .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py -k cli` errors immediately with 'file not found', making every AC line unverifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (or confirm child 4 specifies) how `calibrate_criterion` derives the correct fixture prompt-id for a code-review gate criterion, given that `criterion_prompt_id` hard-codes the `plan-review-` prefix.
evidence: `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42` — `criterion_prompt_id` unconditionally returns `f'plan-review-{criterion_id.replace(".", "-")}'` for every input.
`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:416-421` — `calibrate_criterion` uses `criterion_prompt_id` to derive `prompt_id` for fixture loading; no gate-key branch exists.
Plan is silent on how a code-review criterion's fixture is located when `calibrate_criterion` is given a `project.foo` id that lives under the code-review gate.
findings.finding: `criterion_prompt_id` (used by `calibrate_criterion` to derive the fixture path) always prepends `plan-review-`, mapping `project.foo` → `plan-review-project-foo`. For a code-review gate criterion the correct fixture path would be under a `code-review-project-foo` prompt id, but the plan does not address this prompt-id prefix mismatch when dispatching a code-review criterion to `calibrate_criterion`.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path' / `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42`
scenarios: A code-review project criterion `project.bar` is admitted by the new preflight and dispatched. `calibrate_criterion('project.bar')` derives `prompt_id = 'plan-review-project-bar'` and calls `load_eval_spec('plan-review-project-bar')`, which fails — the fixture lives at `code-review-project-bar` (or equivalent), not under the plan-review prefix.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Approach what interface child 4 adds to `calibrate_criterion` for gate-aware dispatch (e.g. a `gate` keyword, an auto-detect-by-prefix rule, or a separate `calibrate_criterion_code_review` entry point) so the dispatch line in this ticket can be written and tested unambiguously.
evidence: Plan says 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds to calibrate_criterion' but does not name the new parameter/signature.

Current `calibrate_criterion` (eval.py:416-420) unconditionally maps criterion_id → plan-review-<id> prefix via `criterion_prompt_id`, so passing a code-review project criterion id today would produce a `plan-review-project-foo` prompt_id and load the wrong fixture.
The plan says this ticket 'depends on child 4' but child 4's ticket is not accessible; the interface contract is entirely implicit.
findings.finding: The plan's dispatch call (`_eval.calibrate_criterion(args.criterion_id, repo_root=repo_root, runs=args.runs)`) is identical to the current plan-review-only call, but the plan never specifies what interface child 4 adds to `calibrate_criterion` to make it gate-aware. Without knowing whether child 4 adds a `gate` parameter, changes the prompt-id prefix logic, or uses a different dispatch path, this ticket's implementer cannot write the dispatch line correctly or test that a code-review id actually reaches the code-review solver (vs. silently routing through the plan-review path and failing at fixture load).
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'; also Scope: `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py`
scenarios: Implementer writes the dispatch line as `_eval.calibrate_criterion(args.criterion_id, ...)` (mirroring the existing call) without knowing child 4's new parameter; code-review criteria silently route to the plan-review fixture path and fail with a misleading 'fixture not found' error.

Test asserts exit 0 for a code-review project criterion but the calibrate call internally fails because `criterion_prompt_id` still emits `plan-review-project-foo` instead of a code-review-prefixed id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or define) the interface contract that child 4's gate-aware `calibrate_criterion` exposes — specifically: does `criterion_prompt_id` gain a `gate` parameter, what is the code-review fixture path convention (e.g. `code-review-project-<name>.eval.yaml`), and how does the dispatching code in this ticket thread the resolved gate through to `calibrate_criterion` — so the happy-path AC is verifiable without inspecting an unreachable child ticket.
evidence: Plan: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'
ids.py:36-42: `criterion_prompt_id` always returns `f"{PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX}{criterion_id.replace('.', '-')}"` — hardcodes `plan-review-` for ALL gates
eval.py:416-420: `prompt_id = criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id)` called unconditionally in `calibrate_criterion`
eval_solver.py:66-88: `_criterion_id` resolves only against `plan_review.registry.by_id()` — a code-review id would return `None` and fall through
Child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 not found (lookup error), so the gate-aware changes cannot be verified
findings.finding: The plan asserts dispatch to a 'gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds to `calibrate_criterion`', but the current `calibrate_criterion` (eval.py:416-420) calls `criterion_prompt_id()` which unconditionally prepends `plan-review-` to every criterion id regardless of gate — a code-review criterion `project.foo` would map to fixture path `plan-review-project-foo.eval.yaml` (wrong prefix). The child ticket [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] is unresolvable, so the claim that this path 'correctly dispatches' a code-review criterion is an unverified capability assertion.
findings.location: Approach section: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to `calibrate_criterion`'; eval.py:416-420; criteria/ids.py:36-42
scenarios: A code-review criterion `project.foo` admitted by the new preflight is dispatched to `calibrate_criterion`; it resolves to fixture path `plan-review-project-foo.eval.yaml` (wrong gate prefix) instead of the expected `code-review-project-foo.eval.yaml`, causing an `EvalError` on fixture load.
The `_criterion_id` helper in eval_solver.py returns `None` for a code-review id, causing the solve step to fail to recognize the criterion as a calibratable criterion, breaking the happy-path AC.

If `criterion_prompt_id` is extended with a `gate` parameter by child 4, this ticket's preflight dispatch code must pass the correct gate — but the plan specifies no interface contract for how the gate is threaded from admission through to `calibrate_criterion`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Interface contract with child 4' subsection to the Approach that pins: (a) the expected signature change to `calibrate_criterion` (e.g. `gate: str | None = None` parameter), (b) the code-review fixture filename convention, and (c) how the dispatching block in `_criteria_eval` passes the gate. This makes the T1 capability assertion verifiable from the plan alone.

eval.py:414-419: calibrate_criterion uses criterion_prompt_id() which always returns 'plan-review-<id>' — no code-review variant exists

findings.finding: The 'gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds to calibrate_criterion' is referenced as the dispatch target for code-review criteria but its decision protocol — specifically how a project code-review criterion id maps to a prompt_id — is never defined in this plan. The current calibrate_criterion always calls criterion_prompt_id() which emits 'plan-review-*' prefixed ids; a code-review criterion (e.g. project.foo in the code_review gate) would need a different prompt-id mapping, but the plan neither specifies that mapping nor defines what the child ticket must add. The child ticket [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] is unresolvable (returns 404), so the plan cannot be executed as written.

scenarios: An implementor reads this plan and reaches _criteria_eval's dispatch call: they cannot know whether to call calibrate_criterion with a new gate= kwarg, a separate calibrate_code_review_criterion function, or something else.

A code-review project criterion 'project.foo' gets dispatched to calibrate_criterion, which calls criterion_prompt_id('project.foo') → 'plan-review-project-foo', which is the plan-review fixture path — wrong gate, wrong fixture, silent miscalibration.

findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` does not exist in the repository — the plan says 'extend the shared file' but there is no such file to extend; it is a hallucinated edit target.

findings.finding: The physical prompt-id mapping for a code-review project criterion is never defined in this plan or in the codebase. The plan dispatches admitted code-review ids to `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch (child rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8), which in turn calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` per the child ticket — but `criterion_prompt_id` in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` takes no `gate_key` parameter and unconditionally prepends `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX='plan-review-'`. There is no `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant, no `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` function, and no gate-aware overload anywhere in the codebase. The resolver this plan depends on is referenced but structurally undefined.
findings.location: Approach section ('dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path landed on the eval child [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]'); `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py:36-42`; child ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 description
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'code-review-'` to `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` and a gate-aware helper `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str, *, gate_key: str = 'plan_review') -> str` that selects the prefix based on `gate_key`, then reference it explicitly in the Approach section.

findings.finding: The plan's Approach implies criterion_prompt_id already accepts gate_key="code_review" (used by the code-review calibrate branch it dispatches to), but reading src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py confirms the function currently takes only one positional argument and always emits a 'plan-review-' prefix; ticket fc72-bf7e-7652-4228 (which adds gate_key) is still open.
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path'; implicitly relies on criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key="code_review") from [rebar:fc72-bf7e-7652-4228]

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that child ticket [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] is findable and that its changes to `calibrate_criterion` (specifically: a gate parameter or a code-review dispatch arm that handles `project.<name>` code-review criterion ids) are scoped to produce correct behavior before this ticket's tests are written against it.
evidence: Plan: 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'
Ticket rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8 not found in the system (rebar show failed with 'not found')

calibrate_criterion at eval.py:384-430 calls criterion_prompt_id() unconditionally and uses _criterion_solver which invokes the plan-review Pass-1 finder

findings.finding: The plan's dispatch step relies on 'the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion', but that child ticket cannot be resolved (rebar show returns not-found), and the current calibrate_criterion has no code-review dispatch arm: criterion_prompt_id() unconditionally prepends 'plan-review-' to all ids, and _criterion_solver only invokes the plan-review Pass-1 finder. scenarios: This ticket ships before or without child 4's changes: a project.foo code-review criterion passes the new dual-registry preflight, reaches calibrate_criterion, gets mapped to 'plan-review-project-foo', and runs through the plan-review Pass-1 finder — wrong behavior, no error surfaced.
The child ticket's scope is not sufficient to make calibrate_criterion truly gate-aware, leaving the dispatch silently broken.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

findings.finding: The plan asserts `tests/unit/test_code_review_project_criteria.py` was 'created earlier in the stack', but no named dependency actually creates that file — the only cited dependency (`rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8`, child 4) is described as adding the gate-aware calibrate path, not the test file. findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the 'admit iff active in exactly one gate, reject as ambiguous if in both' disambiguation policy (and the rejected --gate alternative) in a persistent artifact (AGENTS.md, an ADR, or the relevant design doc) so future gate additions have a stable decision record.
evidence: Plan documents the rule inline: 'Admit iff present in exactly one gate; dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path'
Plan explicitly rejects the --gate flag alternative with rationale, but only in the plan text itself — not in a persistent artifact
No mention of ADR, AGENTS.md, or design-doc update in Scope section
Scope lists only two files: _llm_eval_commands.py and test_code_review_project_criteria.py
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel cross-component business rule — 'admit iff the id is active in exactly one gate' — that governs how future gate additions will interact with the CLI, but no persistent design artifact (ADR, AGENTS.md entry, or design-doc section) is specified to capture this disambiguation policy.
scenarios: A future engineer adds a third gate (e.g., 'integration-review') and must re-derive the disambiguation policy from code rather than a design doc
A reviewer questions why --gate was not used and finds no persistent rationale
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief AGENTS.md or design-doc entry under a 'criteria eval gate resolution' heading describing the one-gate rule and the rationale for rejecting the --gate flag. Reference it from the Scope section of this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for why gate resolution belongs in the CLI preflight (e.g. 'early clear-message rejection before fixture I/O, and non-CLI callers to `calibrate_criterion` go through child 4's gate-aware path directly') rather than solely justifying it by contrast with the --gate flag.
evidence: Rejected alternative section states the --gate flag is 'heavier UX for a rare case' — this is a rationale against a flag, not a positive rationale for CLI-layer gate resolution.

An alternative placement (gate resolution inside `calibrate_criterion`) is not addressed; the plan offers no argument for why CLI is preferable to encapsulating the dual-registry lookup inside `calibrate_criterion` where non-CLI callers would also benefit.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for placing the dual-registry lookup in the CLI preflight (rather than inside `calibrate_criterion` itself). The 'Rejected alternative' section only argues against a `--gate` flag; it does not explain why the CLI layer — rather than a lower-level encapsulation — is the correct home for gate resolution.
scenarios: A future MCP tool or library caller invokes `calibrate_criterion` directly with a code-review id and gets the plan-review error, because gate resolution lives in the CLI only.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Approach: 'The CLI preflight is the right layer for the ambiguity rejection message (it names both gates to the operator); the gate-aware calibrate_criterion path added by child 4 handles the case where calibrate_criterion is called directly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Approach section whether a project code-review criterion must be activated under the gate-specific 'code_review' overlay key (not the shared 'activate' key) to be single-gate, and confirm that a 'project.<name>' id can appear in exactly one gate's effective_criteria() in the supported configuration.
evidence: Plan: 'Look the id up in BOTH the plan-review effective registry and the code-review effective registry'
criteria/overlay.py:6: '.rebar/criteria_routing.json can carry a per-gate map — {"plan_review": {…}, "code_review": {…}, "activate": [...]}'
criteria/ids.py: PROJECT_PREFIX = 'project.' — same namespace for both gates
Plan does not define a distinguishing namespace or prefix for code-review-only project criteria
findings.finding: The 'one-gate resolution' decision rule is stated but the namespace identity of a project code-review criterion id is never defined. plan_review uses 'project.<name>' as its project-criterion namespace; code_review also uses 'project.<name>' via the same shared overlay mechanism (criteria/overlay.py). An id like 'project.foo' could therefore be legitimately active in BOTH gates simultaneously — which the plan calls 'ambiguous' — but the plan never defines what a project code-review criterion id looks like when it is NOT also a plan-review id, i.e., when the one-gate path is actually reachable in practice. This makes the happy-path scenario structurally ambiguous.
findings.location: Approach section: 'Admit iff present in exactly one gate'
scenarios: A project activates 'project.foo' in the 'activate' list of criteria_routing.json, which adds it to both gates' effective_criteria(). The CLI then rejects it as ambiguous even though the user intended a code-review criterion — the one-gate happy path is never reachable via the shared overlay activate key.
Test authors are unsure whether to activate a criterion under 'code_review' gate_key only (via gate-specific key) or 'activate' (shared), because the plan never specifies.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence such as: 'A project code-review criterion is activated via the `code_review` gate key in `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`, NOT via the shared `activate` key, so it appears only in code_review.registry.effective_criteria() and not in plan_review.registry.by_id(); this is what makes single-gate resolution reachable.'

findings.finding: The Scope section promises a help-text update ('Update the subparser help text to include a code-review example alongside the plan-review one'), but no Acceptance Criterion covers it and no proving command verifies it. The -k cli selector over the named test file will not catch a missing or wrong help-text change.

findings.finding: The help-text update ('Update the subparser help text to include a code-review example alongside the plan-review one') is described in the Approach and implied by the Scope but has no covering acceptance criterion.
findings.location: Approach (last bullet) / Scope (`_llm_eval_commands.py` — 'help text') / Acceptance Criteria (neither AC1 nor AC2 verifies help text)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] The `criteria eval` subparser help text contains a code-review id example; verified by inspecting the parser's `--help` output in the unit test or by a docstring assertion.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Approach (or Scope) section that LLMError from the code-review registry lookup is caught and reported as a clean 'Error: …' message (exit 1), consistent with the existing plan-review error handler at lines 153-155.
evidence: Existing code _llm_eval_commands.py:153-155 catches LLMError from plan-review registry only
Plan mentions catching LLMError only in the context of the existing preflight block, not the new code-review registry call
code_review/registry.py:179-184: effective_criteria() delegates to shared criteria mechanism which can raise on a bad overlay
findings.finding: The plan specifies exception handling for the LLMError from the plan-review registry lookup (existing lines 153-155) but never addresses what happens if code_review.registry.effective_criteria(repo_root) itself raises — e.g., due to a malformed overlay file. The fallback for an incomplete/failed code-review registry sub-step is unspecified.
findings.location: Approach section: 'Look the id up in BOTH the plan-review effective registry and the code-review effective registry'
scenarios: A project has a malformed .rebar/criteria_routing.json under the code_review key; effective_criteria() raises LLMError; the new preflight code has no catch, producing an unhandled traceback instead of a clean CLI error.
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'Wrap both registry lookups in a single try/except LLMError block (or extend the existing one) so a bad code-review overlay also produces a clean error message rather than a traceback.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
